title: Build SHA control-plane foundation, enrollment contracts, and dashboard shell
description: Why now: SHA needs one credible product slice that Jenny and specialist agents can execute autonomously without wandering. This task creates the monorepo foundation for the control plane, the first typed contracts for endpoint enrollment, posture uploads, installer profiles, and approval grants, and the dashboard shell operators will use to manage SHA agents and future hardening workflows.
done_when: The repo has a runnable backend skeleton and frontend shell wired to SHA-specific routes and branding.
Typed backend contract models exist for endpoint enrollment, posture snapshot upload, installer profiles, and approval grants.

The initial persistence model exists for endpoints, posture snapshots, posture results, installer profiles, and approval grants; posture results link back to endpoints via stable endpoint IDs and control_key strings.
Minimal API surface exists for enrollment, posture uploads, installer-profile create/list shells, and approval-grant create/list shells with typed request/response models.

Dashboard shell includes navigation and placeholder screens for fleet, endpoint detail, controls, approvals, and installer package generation, and focused verification covers contract validity plus route/nav presence.
objective: Stand up the first real SHA product slice so future autonomous work can build against a stable backend/frontend/agent contract instead of more planning-only artifacts.
spirit_anti: Do not build real remediation logic, arbitrary remote execution, or full compliance ingestion in this task. Do not add macOS support, live scraping, or broad admin shells. Keep the slice structural and execution-ready.
constraints: Keep SHAna scoped to hardening-management surfaces; do not introduce broad endpoint shell capability.
Use FastAPI + Pydantic 2 + SQLAlchemy 2 on Python 3.13.12, with SQLite as the only required persistence engine for this first slice and no Alembic requirement yet.
`uv` 0.10.10 is already available on the host and is the required Python package/bootstrap tool for this slice.

Use Next.js App Router on Node 24.14.1 with pnpm 10.32.1, with TypeScript enabled, and use Vitest plus Testing Library for frontend verification. `package.json` must map `build` to `next build` and `test` to a non-watch Vitest entrypoint.
Frontend must compile and build successfully without the backend running; all first-slice data comes from local typed fixtures only.
Frontend may define local TypeScript view-model types in `frontend/lib/api.ts`, but those types must be limited to the exact fields pinned in this plan.
Backend Pydantic models in `backend/app/schemas/contracts.py` are the only source of truth for the contract layer in this slice.
Do not require a compiled Go toolchain in this task because `go` is not installed on the host yet; represent the agent boundary with contracts and docs only.

Backend dependency versions are fixed in `backend/pyproject.toml`: `fastapi==0.128.1`, `pydantic==2.12.5`, `pydantic-settings==2.12.0`, `sqlalchemy==2.0.46`, `uvicorn[standard]==0.40.0`, `httpx==0.28.1`, and `pytest==9.0.2`.
The backend task must also create and commit `backend/uv.lock` so the exact Python dependency set is reproducible under Python 3.13.12.

Frontend dependency versions are fixed in `frontend/package.json`: `next==16.1.7`, `react==19.2.3`, `react-dom==19.2.3`, `typescript==5.9.3`, `vitest==4.1.0`, `@testing-library/react==16.3.2`, `@testing-library/jest-dom==6.9.1`, `jsdom==27.4.0`, `@types/react==19.2.14`, and `@types/react-dom==19.2.3`.
The frontend task must also create and commit `frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml` so the exact Node dependency set is reproducible under Node 24.14.1 and pnpm 10.32.1.
Backend boot contract: `uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010` from `backend/`; frontend boot contract: `pnpm dev --port 3010` from `frontend/`.
Enrollment semantics: server issues the stable endpoint_id; repeated enrollment keyed by agent fingerprint returns 200 and updates the same endpoint record rather than creating duplicates.
Endpoint `status` enum is fixed to `pending`, `active`, and `stale` in this slice.
Endpoint `platform` enum is fixed to `windows` and `linux` in this slice.

Health endpoint response is fixed to `{"status":"ok","service":"sha-backend","version":"0.1.0"}`. The `--reload` boot command is a local-dev convenience only; closeout must still prove the FastAPI app imports and serves this exact body through tests without depending on reload behavior.

All API timestamp fields (`last_seen_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `observed_at`, `expires_at`) serialize as ISO8601 UTC strings ending in `Z`. Inbound `observed_at` and `expires_at` may include any valid timezone offset, but the backend must normalize them to UTC before persistence and before returning API responses.

Backend persistence in this slice is real SQLite persistence, not in-memory placeholders. The task must create SQLite tables for `endpoints`, `posture_snapshots`, `posture_results`, `installer_profiles`, and `approval_grants`, and collection routes must read those tables.
Backend startup must create the `backend/data` directory if missing and auto-create the required SQLite tables on app startup or test bootstrap without a separate migration step.

Posture upload semantics: one snapshot request contains many control results and must include endpoint_id, observed_at, platform_profile, and `results[]` items with `control_key`, status, and evidence summary fields. Duplicate `control_key` values inside one snapshot are invalid using case-insensitive comparison, while duplicate keys across different snapshots are allowed.

Posture snapshot success responses are fixed to `{"snapshot_id": string, "endpoint_id": string, "observed_at": utc-string, "accepted_result_count": integer, "created_at": utc-string}`. Successful POST /api/posture-snapshots returns 202, persists one snapshot row plus one result row per accepted result, and `accepted_result_count` must equal the number of persisted result rows for that snapshot.

Enrollment request fields are fixed to `agent_fingerprint`, `hostname`, `platform`, `platform_version`, `agent_version`, optional `tenant_id`, and optional `site_id`. `agent_fingerprint`, `hostname`, `platform`, and `agent_version` are required non-empty strings after trim; `agent_fingerprint` is normalized by trim + lowercase for idempotency checks; `platform_version`, `tenant_id`, and `site_id` are optional nullable strings. Successful create and successful same-fingerprint re-enrollment both return endpoint `status=active`. Endpoint response objects always include `platform_version`, `tenant_id`, and `site_id` keys, using explicit JSON null when values are absent. Re-enrollment for the same fingerprint must preserve `endpoint_id` and `created_at`; omitted optional fields preserve existing values, explicit JSON `null` clears them, and if the same fingerprint is submitted with a different `platform` enum than the stored record, the request fails with 409 body `{"detail":"agent fingerprint already enrolled for a different platform"}` instead of silently changing platform families.

Global string normalization rule for this slice: required string inputs are trimmed before validation and are stored/returned in trimmed form; `agent_fingerprint` is additionally lowercased after trim for storage and matching; installer `name` preserves interior whitespace but not leading/trailing whitespace; approval `endpoint_ids` are trimmed before duplicate/nonexistent validation and stored/returned trimmed; optional nullable strings remain null when omitted or explicitly cleared.

Endpoint IDs are server-generated strings matching `^ep_[0-9a-f]{32}$`; posture snapshot IDs match `^snap_[0-9a-f]{32}$`; installer profile IDs match `^ip_[0-9a-f]{32}$`; approval grant IDs match `^grant_[0-9a-f]{32}$`.

SQLite persistence must enforce uniqueness with real database constraints, not app logic only: `endpoints.endpoint_id` unique, normalized `endpoints.agent_fingerprint` unique, `installer_profiles.id` unique, normalized installer `(platform,name)` unique, and `approval_grants.approval_grant_id` unique.

Installer profile objects returned by create and list routes are fixed to `{id, name, platform, channel, control_plane_url, policy_mode, tenant_id, site_id, created_at, updated_at}` with explicit nulls for missing tenant/site values. `id` is server-generated on create, profile `name` must be unique per `platform` after trim + lowercase normalization, blank or whitespace-only names fail with 422, GET list returns `{"items": [...]}` using those same full objects ordered by `created_at` ascending then `id` ascending, duplicate `(platform,name)` creates fail with 409 body `{"detail":"installer profile already exists for platform"}`, and `control_plane_url` must be an absolute http/https URL that is stored and returned exactly as validated without custom trailing-slash rewriting.

Installer profile name normalization uses trim + lowercase only; internal repeated whitespace remains significant, so `Foo Bar` and `Foo  Bar` are distinct names if their trimmed/lowercased forms still differ exactly.

Approval grant objects returned by create and list routes are fixed to `{approval_grant_id, endpoint_ids, allowed_actions, requested_by, approved_by, reason, expires_at, status, created_at, updated_at}`. Approval grants must use explicit arrays for `endpoint_ids` and `allowed_actions`; no wildcard scopes in this first slice. Allowed actions are fixed to `collect_security_context`, `inspect_control`, `apply_control`, `rollback_control`, and `request_elevated_troubleshooting`. `approval_grant_id` is server-generated, `requested_by`, `approved_by`, and `reason` are required non-empty strings after trim, `expires_at` is required, `status` enum in this slice is fixed to `approved|expired|revoked`, `status` is set to `approved` on successful create, duplicate `endpoint_ids` after trim-only normalization or duplicate `allowed_actions` by exact enum value are rejected with 422, nonexistent endpoint IDs are rejected with 422 before duplicate checks, GET list returns `{"items": [...]}` using those same full objects ordered by `created_at` ascending then `approval_grant_id` ascending, and timestamps serialize as ISO8601 UTC strings.

Posture snapshot create requests require `results` length >= 1; empty arrays are rejected with 422. Successful persistence stores trimmed `control_key` values while preserving original case from the request. Every result row must persist `control_key`, `status`, nullable `current_value`, nullable `recommended_value`, nullable `severity`, required `evidence_summary`, and `reboot_required` so round-trip tests can assert the stored payload.

This first slice does not require any backend endpoint list/detail API. Frontend fleet and endpoint-detail pages use local typed fixtures only; README and tests must not imply `/api/endpoints` collection or detail GET routes exist yet.

README closeout contract: root README must document local setup commands for backend (`uv sync`, `uv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010`) and frontend (`pnpm install`, `pnpm dev --port 3010`), note that the frontend uses local fixtures and builds without a running backend, and explicitly state that `/api/endpoints` list/detail routes are not part of this first slice.

Placeholder API responses should use concrete JSON objects or `{"items": [...]}` list envelopes. In this slice only collection routes are required; item GET/PATCH/DELETE routes are explicitly out of scope.

Frontend view-model types in `frontend/lib/api.ts` must mirror only the pinned fields from this plan. If backend models later gain extra fields, this slice ignores them rather than inventing drift-control code or generated schema export.
External old-work references under `/home/kasadis/references/old_work/` are optional inspiration only and cannot be required for normal-path implementation.
Task is not execution-ready until task_shape critique passes on the imported task.
testing_strategy: Add focused backend tests for health, contract models, SQLite persistence round-trips, collection envelope shapes, unknown-endpoint handling, enrollment idempotency, fingerprint trim/lowercase normalization, enroll 409 on cross-platform fingerprint reuse, omitted-vs-null optional enrollment updates, posture snapshot persistence/ack counts, posture empty-results rejection, installer duplicate-name conflicts, approval duplicate-array validation with nonexistent-endpoint precedence, deterministic collection ordering, required-string whitespace rejection, and UTC timestamp normalization.
Add focused frontend Vitest coverage for dashboard nav, required route shells, and successful `next build` under the pinned Node/pnpm runtime.
Run `uv run --directory backend pytest tests -q`, `pnpm test --run`, `pnpm build`, and then `dt --check` if repo tooling exists; otherwise report the exact dt blocker separately.

risks: If the monorepo scaffold grows too broad, autonomous execution will stall in setup churn instead of shipping the first slice.
Approval and endpoint contract shapes will influence later agent execution boundaries, so keep them strict and typed.
Go toolchain is not currently available on the host, so the first slice must avoid compile-time dependence on a real agent binary.
Frontend overreach is a risk; placeholder shells are enough for this task.

subtasks.description: Create the backend skeleton, domain models, and initial API contracts.

steps.description: Create the FastAPI app, configuration, SQLite wiring, and health endpoint.

spec.detail: The backend must boot cleanly with SHA branding and a health response `{"status":"ok","service":"sha-backend","version":"0.1.0"}` on path `/health` and port 8010. SQLite lives at `backend/data/sha.sqlite3`, and the task is responsible for creating the parent data directory, keeping the DB file ignored from git, and auto-creating tables for endpoints, posture snapshots, posture results, installer profiles, and approval grants without a separate migration step.

steps.description: Define the canonical endpoint, posture snapshot, installer profile, and approval grant models before wiring routes.

spec.detail: Endpoint object fields are `endpoint_id`, `agent_fingerprint`, `hostname`, `platform`, `platform_version`, `agent_version`, `tenant_id?`, `site_id?`, `status`, `last_seen_at`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`, with `status` limited to `pending|active|stale`. Enrollment POST request shape is fixed to `{agent_fingerprint, hostname, platform, platform_version?, agent_version, tenant_id?, site_id?}`. First enrollment returns 201 with the full endpoint object and `status=active`; same-fingerprint re-enrollment returns 200 with `status=active` and updates `hostname`, `platform_version`, `agent_version`, `tenant_id`, `site_id`, `last_seen_at`, and `updated_at` while preserving `endpoint_id`, `platform`, and `created_at`. Omitted optional fields preserve stored values, explicit null clears them, and re-enrollment with a different `platform` than the stored record fails with 409.

steps.description: Add posture, installer-profile, and approval-grant contract details to the backend models.

spec.detail: Posture snapshot POST accepts one `endpoint_id`, one `observed_at`, one `platform_profile`, and `results[]` entries with `control_key`, `status`, `current_value?`, `recommended_value?`, `severity?`, `evidence_summary`, and `reboot_required`. `control_key` duplicate detection trims surrounding whitespace and compares case-insensitively, so `SSH-ROOT` and ` ssh-root ` are duplicates. Allowed posture `status` values are `pass`, `fail`, `warn`, `error`, and `not_applicable`. Unknown endpoint IDs on posture POST intentionally return 404 because the route addresses one concrete endpoint-owned resource. Installer profiles are collection-only records with `id`, `name`, `platform` enum `windows|linux`, `channel` enum `stable|preview`, `control_plane_url`, `policy_mode` enum `observe|safe_auto|approval_required`, and optional tenant/site metadata; installer duplicate checks use trim + lowercase only. Approval grants are collection-only records with `approval_grant_id`, `endpoint_ids[]` min length 1, `allowed_actions[]` min length 1 from the fixed action enum, required non-empty `requested_by`, `approved_by`, `reason`, required `expires_at`, `status` enum `approved|expired|revoked`, and timestamps; create requests reject nonexistent endpoint IDs with 422 because validation happens over the submitted endpoint set before any grant record is created, duplicate `endpoint_ids` compare after trim-only normalization, and duplicate `allowed_actions` compare by exact enum value.

steps.description: Add placeholder API routes for POST /api/endpoints/enroll, POST /api/posture-snapshots, GET/POST /api/installer-profiles, and GET/POST /api/approval-grants.

spec.detail: Use concrete status codes and bodies: enroll POST returns 201 endpoint object on first create and 200 on same-fingerprint updates, posture POST returns 202 ack `{snapshot_id, endpoint_id, observed_at, accepted_result_count, created_at}`, installer/approval list GETs return `{"items": [...]}` in deterministic order, installer/approval create POSTs return 201 created object, unknown endpoint IDs on posture POST return 404, approval POST rejects nonexistent endpoint IDs with 422, and FastAPI default 422 is acceptable for remaining validation failures.

subtasks.description: Create the dashboard shell and initial operator views.

steps.description: Create the Next.js App Router shell with SHA navigation and branded landing page.

spec.detail: Use static typed mock data only. `frontend/lib/api.ts` should expose the minimal local view-model types and fixtures required by the page shells, and the dynamic endpoint-detail shell must render the route param in a stable heading or identifier.
Installer UI must make room for Windows/Linux profile selection and future package generation, but can stop at typed placeholder actions in this slice.

subtasks.description: Create the SHA agent boundary docs and repo-local contract notes.

steps.description: Document the planned SHA agent contract, capability verbs, and approval-dependent elevated troubleshooting boundary in repo-local docs.

spec.detail: Do not require a compiled agent binary in this task; capture the contract cleanly so a later Go implementation can follow it.

steps.description: Record the exact backend contract fields and action enums the future agent must honor.

spec.detail: Keep this as documentation only in the first slice; shared exported schema artifacts are deferred to a follow-up task once the backend contracts settle.

subtasks.description: Verify the foundation slice and lock it with focused tests.

steps.description: Add focused tests for backend health/contracts, SQLite round-trips, enrollment idempotency, unknown-endpoint handling, collection envelope shapes, and practical frontend route/nav rendering.

spec.detail: Tests must prove first enroll returns 201 and re-enroll returns 200 with exact full endpoint objects including explicit null keys, same-fingerprint re-enrollment reuses one endpoint record, health returns the fixed JSON body on `/health`, startup auto-creates `backend/data` plus the required SQLite tables, posture POST success persists one snapshot row plus matching result rows and returns the same `accepted_result_count`, posture POST returns 404 for unknown endpoints, 422 for duplicate `control_key` values inside one snapshot, and 422 for empty `results`, enroll returns 409 for same-fingerprint cross-platform reuse, omitted enrollment optional fields preserve prior values while explicit null clears them, installer profiles reject duplicate `(platform,name)` after trim + lowercase normalization, installer and approval empty list responses return `{"items": []}`, installer and approval create responses match list object shapes field-for-field, installer and approval list ordering remains deterministic even when `created_at` timestamps collide, approval grants reject empty or duplicate `endpoint_ids` / `allowed_actions` arrays and nonexistent endpoint IDs with 422, timestamps normalize to UTC `Z` strings, and the dynamic `/endpoints/[endpointId]` route renders a stable shell without backend fetches during build.

steps.description: Run `uv run --directory backend pytest tests -q`, `pnpm test --run`, `pnpm build`, and then `dt --check` if repo tooling exists.

spec.detail: If `dt --check` still is not wired, report that exact blocker separately, but the direct backend/frontend verification commands above remain mandatory for closeout. `pnpm build` closeout must prove the App Router pages do not require backend imports, environment secrets, or network fetches at build time.
